rag_rules: Preserve AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component type or implementation scopes
Place component type contracts in type annexes; use implementation annexes for implementation-level bindings
Avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Use concrete bounds, named constants, or visible relations for input envelope assumptions and output range guarantees
generic_rules: Insert annex blocks in the most specific valid scope to maintain architecture clarity
Use AGREE scalar types int, real, and bool for declarations
Avoid range comparisons or 0/1 equality over Boolean, enumeration-like, or opaque data ports
Align helper declaration types with the expression to prevent type mismatches
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations
Use nested if ... then ... else for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators for generated contracts
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
Do not place contracts in invalid scopes
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
